fonds.institution: Whyte Museum of the Canadian Rockies
fonds.scope: Annotated copy of 'Wild Flowers of the North American Mountains' with dates of observations and notes
fonds.findings: Henshaw's personal annotated copy of her own wildflower book, with observation dates and field notes marking locations of specimens collected.
fonds.notes: Reference Code M67/50. Personal working copy with handwritten annotations.

fonds.institution: Whyte Museum of the Canadian Rockies
fonds.scope: Photographs of Julia Henshaw in mountaineering contexts (1910-1911)
fonds.findings: Glass negatives showing Henshaw on tote road near Glacier, BC with horses and Mary Schaffer (1910-1911). While slightly later than 1906, these document her continued Alpine work and mountaineering partnership with Mary Schaffer in Selkirk Mountains.
fonds.notes: Reference Codes: V653/NG-909, V653/NG-908, V653/NG-907. Three documented images of Henshaw with Schaffer in the Rockies.

fonds.institution: Whyte Museum of the Canadian Rockies
fonds.scope: Records related to Julia Henshaw's role in the Alpine Club of Canada
fonds.findings: 1 record in ACC fonds related to Henshaw's activities; likely includes 1906 Yoho camp documentation.
fonds.notes: Direct link to ACC archives at Whyte Museum; physical access recommended for original camp records.

fonds.institution: Whyte Museum of the Canadian Rockies
fonds.scope: Biographical clippings and press file on Julia Henshaw
fonds.findings: Compiled biographical newsfile containing contemporary press coverage of her mountain explorations and botanical work.
fonds.notes: Call Number: Biofile. Contains period newspaper and magazine articles documenting her public profile.

fonds.institution: Whyte Museum of the Canadian Rockies
fonds.scope: Published postcard series of Henshaw's wildflower illustrations, published by Raphael Tuck & Sons, London
fonds.findings: Documented postcard of 'Rocky Mountain Wildflowers, No. 5. Anemone' (white flowers). Part of commercial publication of Henshaw's botanical illustrations. Whyte donor note states Henshaw images appear in 1915 publication.
fonds.notes: Call Number V151/PG-R13-30e. Published London: Raphael Tuck & Sons, Ltd. Accession 7979. Documents commercial distribution of her botanical work.

fonds.institution: Whyte Museum of the Canadian Rockies
fonds.scope: Photographic records and biographical material on women mountaineers
fonds.findings: Related reference materials on women's mountaineering in the Canadian Rockies including Henshaw.
fonds.notes: M107 series; requires in-person archival research.

fonds.institution: Whyte Museum of the Canadian Rockies
fonds.scope: Secondary sources including biography and women's mountaineering history
fonds.findings: Two key published works: (1) 'Julia: a biography of Julia W. Henshaw' by Michael Kluckner (2018, Whyte reference 08.3 Kl66j) - graphic biography covering her life from Montreal to Vancouver to France; (2) 'This wild spirit: women in the Rocky Mountains of Canada' edited by Colleen Skidmore (2006, G512 S55) - includes Henshaw as one of featured women mountaineers of the Rocky Mountains.
fonds.notes: These are published secondary sources held by Whyte Library; primary archival materials require direct archive access.

leads_pending_manual_access.reason: National biographical files, press coverage, WWI Red Cross service records
Full-text digitization of 'Mountain Wildflowers of America' (1906) and 'Wild Flowers of the North American Mountains' (1915) - out of copyright works

search_log.result: 31 records found; 6 direct Henshaw items + 25 related records. Key finding: M67/V225 (annotated wildflowers book), M107/V653 (photographs with Schaffer 1910-11), biographical file, ACC fonds reference
Whyte Museum verified as primary repository. Alpine Club of Canada materials require in-person access at Whyte.
Archive.org pages require JavaScript; attempted direct URL access unsuccessful. Recommended: direct search on archive.org for 'mountainwildflow' or similar identifiers
Site accessible but requires direct online search for biographical/press files
